---
title: "Unit 7 Iris Classification-Naive Bayes"
author: "Isabella Westlauer"
date: "2026-02-11"
output: html_document
---
For the full (multinomial) IRIS data (the iris dataset in R), do a 70-30 train/test cross validation and use sepal length and width as predictors.  

```{r}
library(caret)
library(tidyr)
library(plyr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(e1071)
summary(iris)
iris_selected <- select(iris, "Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Species")
# head(iris_selected)
# sum(is.na(iris_selected$Sepal.Width))
```


Generate 100 different train/test splits and calculate the average accuracy, sensitivity and specificity. 

```{r}
iterations = 100
masterAcc = numeric(iterations) #initialize a vector of 100
masterSens = numeric(iterations)
masterSpec = numeric(iterations)

splitPerc = .7 #Training / Test split Percentage

for(j in 1:iterations)
{
  #do not set seed so this is a different train-test split every time
  trainIndices = sample(1:dim(iris_selected)[1],round(splitPerc * dim(iris_selected)[1]))
  train = iris_selected[trainIndices,]
  test = iris_selected[-trainIndices,]
  
  model = naiveBayes(train[,c(1,2)],train$Species)
  table(predict(model,test[,c(1,2)]),test$Species)
  CM = confusionMatrix(table(predict(model,test[,c(1,2)]),test$Species))
  masterAcc[j] = CM$overall[1]
  masterSens[j] = CM$byClass[1]
  masterSpec[j] = CM$byClass[2]
}

MeanAcc = mean(masterAcc) # 0.7753333
MeanSens = mean(masterSens) # 0.9779298
MeanSpec = mean(masterSpec) # 0.7227796

df <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(value = masterAcc, metric = "Accuracy"),
  data.frame(value = masterSens, metric = "Sensitivity"),
  data.frame(value = masterSpec, metric = "Specificity")
)
means <- df %>%
  group_by(metric) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value))

ggplot(df, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = means,
             aes(xintercept = mean_value),
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "blue",
             linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Performance Metrics",
       x = "Value",
       y = "Count")




```

Compare the average accuracy to that to the KNN model you used in Unit 6.  

```{r}

```
